codeReview.summary: The call-feed-filters-search feature has been successfully implemented with all required functionality: Status filter (All/Answered/Voicemail), Date range filter (Today/This Week/This Month/Custom with date pickers), Search by name/phone (case-insensitive partial match), combined filtering with intersection logic, empty state with 'Clear filters' action, and reset behavior. The implementation is well-structured, type-safe, and thoroughly tested.

issues.description: The `matchesDateRange` function could be memoized for performance optimization with large datasets. Currently, the filtering logic runs on every render even when filter criteria haven't changed. The outer `useMemo` for `filteredCalls` already helps, but individual filter predicates could also benefit from memoization.

Test file does not include explicit edge case test for null/undefined date handling in custom date range filtering. While the code handles null dates, there is no explicit test verifying this behavior in the custom date range context.

The custom date range filter does not validate that end date is after start date. Users could theoretically select a start date after the end date, which would return no results. While this is handled gracefully (shows empty state), a user-facing warning could improve UX.

The `parseDateInput` function uses `Number.parseInt` without specifying radix 10 explicitly. While the default behavior works correctly for the expected 'yyyy-mm-dd' format, it is a best practice to always specify the radix to avoid edge cases with leading zeros.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker properly followed AGENTS.md conventions: used Tailwind CSS with shadcn/ui patterns, handled null/undefined values (normalizes to 'N/A' via parent CallRow component), used `cn()` utility from shadcn for conditional classes (in CallRow, referenced in this feature), and followed the file structure conventions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: call-feed.tsx:93-107 - empty state handling, call-feed.tsx:33-61 - null handling in filtering functions, uses Tailwind classes consistently throughout.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the skill procedure with minor deviation: used `npm run build && npm run start` instead of `npm run dev` for manual verification due to Windows environment stability issues with detached `next dev` sessions. This deviation was justified and documented in skillFeedback.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure: false with documented deviation for manual verification approach; all other skill steps (tests first, implement, typecheck, lint, agent-browser verification) were followed correctly.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The date filtering implementation correctly handles timezone edge cases by using local date parsing with `new Date(year, month-1, day)` rather than ISO string parsing, which is important since the call data uses local date-time strings. This pattern could be worth documenting in architecture.md for future date-related features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: call-feed.tsx:68-74 - parseDateInput uses local constructor, matchesDateRange:85-89 - uses startOfDay to normalize comparison dates to local midnight.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly identified that the live data currently has `callStatus: null` for all rows (pre-existing data issue), and documented this as a non-blocking discovered issue with a suggested fix. This demonstrates proper handling of environment-specific data issues during manual verification.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.discoveredIssues[0]: 'Pre-existing: live /api/calls data currently returns callStatus: null for available rows, so manual verification with real answered/voicemail rows is not possible in this environment.'

summary: Feature review: PASS. The call-feed-filters-search implementation fully satisfies all requirements from the feature description and validation assertions (VAL-FEED-005, VAL-FEED-006, VAL-FEED-007, VAL-FEED-008, VAL-FEED-010, VAL-FEED-011, VAL-FEED-016). The code is well-structured, properly typed, follows established patterns, and has comprehensive test coverage (9 tests covering status filtering, preset date ranges, custom date ranges, search by name/phone, combined filtering, empty state, and reset behavior). All validators pass (tests, typecheck, lint). Minor non-blocking issues identified: potential memoization optimization opportunity, missing explicit null-date test case, lack of date range validation UX, and parseInt radix specification. No blocking issues found.
